title: PostgreSQL Work Claims, SKIP LOCKED, Leases, and Fencing · Go Semantics Case: Feed Go Lease Token
summary: Multiple instances poll one source, row locks span network calls, expired workers commit late results, leases renew silently after ownership loss, and queue fairness is unknown. Predict the reader decision, feed or API contract, Go and PostgreSQL ownership, bounded failure, and observable evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the feed go lease token increment in multi-instance fenced fetch-claim protocol. Learners progress through prediction, explicit th…
objectives: Issue attempt and fencing tokens with owner, expiry, heartbeat budget, and state so stale workers cannot commit.
Claim a bounded due batch with ordered FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED work in a short transaction and release locks before network I/O.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Predict Feed Go Lease Token · Theory Feed Go Lease Token
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go team handles case 2334 during a publisher-fairness incident rehearsal. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove short SKIP LOCKED claims, fenced leases, conditional finalize, and fault recovery; untrusted XML and feed content must not cross admission or rendering boundaries; then change one RSS, Atom, namespace, date, identity, content, or encoding case and reconcile provenance. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Issue attempt and fencing tokens with owner, expiry, heartbeat budget, and state so stale workers cannot commit.
